bullets: PP price drop paused as sellers held vs late last week.
Chinese stocks high; ASEAN duty-free goods undercut Chinese exports.
Sabic June PE finals failed to attract vs cheaper ME/Asia offers.
US-origin LDPE (Chevron/Solmer) at 1,220 CIF drew interest.
Buyers stayed out; Chinese SG-5 to 22,000 in the North.
Veterans see prices still falling, if more slowly.

editorial.changedNote: Mostly sideways; a few HDPE/PVC items eased

summary: Asian PE stagnated or fell on expected ME supply and competitive Chinese exports.
Sabic June PE finals drew few buyers as ME and Asian rivals priced lower.
Domestic sellers held early-week offers but slow trade kept pressure down.
paras: Per ICIS, last week LDPE in Vietnam sideways at 1,450-1,550 CFR; LLDPE 1,150-1,250 (high end -30); HDPE Film -20 to 1,230-1,330; HDPE injection/blow flat at 1,200-1,300.
Easing US-Iran tensions raised ME supply expectations. China PE export prices and volumes fell; SEA leaned on Chinese supply though volume dropped.

On May 30 Sabic finalized June LDPE/LLDPE ex-Singapore: LDPE Film 1,540, LDPE injection HP200023 1,600, coating 1,620-1,630, LLDPE Film 1,200-1,220, LLDPE roto 1,400-1,450, mLLDPE C6 1,580; earlier HDPE Film 1,320, blow B5428 1,360-1,420, injection M200056 1,600.
Few signed Sabic HDPE as other ME finals were below 1,300, Chinese HDPE Film 1,250-1,290, and a Prefchem (Malaysia) HDPE lot won at only 1,230.
US-origin LDPE (Chevron/Solmer) at 1,220 CIF (down 50), the market low, drew interest; Tricon LLDPE 1,100; Exxon off-grade LLDPE 1,050 CFR, mLLDPE 1,100.

Domestic LLDPE 7042 commonly 34,500-35,500; ME 36,500-37,000. Sinopec HDPE blow 5502 at 38,500; ME HDPE Film 39,500-41,000; China/US/Russia HDPE 38,000-39,000. LDPE premium 49,000-50,000, US-origin 47,000-48,000.

summary: Vietnamese buyers limited new PP imports on high leftover stock and a domestic-supply preference.
Chinese polyolefin stocks stayed high as freight made exports uncompetitive vs ASEAN duty-free goods.
Domestic PP drop paused as sellers held, though supplier gaps persisted.
paras: Per ICIS on May 29, Vietnam PP yarn/injection was 1,190-1,230 CFR, sideways at the low end and -20 at the high end.
Imports stayed limited as buyers prioritized flexible domestic supply; March-April panic buying left ample plant inventory. Some traders cut prices to move stock.
In China, state-maker PP/PE stocks ran above last year; higher freight made Chinese goods less competitive, especially after ASEAN duty-free prices fell. June restarts will add supply.
On June 1, the PP drop paused: in the North, NSRP distributors held off new 114G/105G; commercial offers ~35,500, lowest finalized 35,200.
Cheapest was Chinese PP: yarn T03 ~34,500, copo K8003 ~38,000, sideways.
In the South, NSRP/BSR paused drops while other-origin PP eased; gaps stayed wide (114G/T3034 35,900 to 37,000). Hyosung F501 ~36,700; LSP P400S 38,500. Off-grade copo to 34,500.

summary: Asian PVC kept falling on weak oil cost support and slow Chinese destocking.
Regional buyers limited purchases, drawing down earlier-hoarded stockpiles.
Vietnam stayed stagnant; sellers discounted Chinese and American PVC with little success.
paras: In China, despite maintenance and upstream cuts, PVC inventory stayed high vs last year; slow destocking held back recovery.
Across Asia, PVC fell again. Optimism on US-Iran talks and a possible Hormuz reopening cut oil prices, weakening oil-based cost support. Buyers held March-hoarded stock and bought small.
In Vietnam, trade stayed quiet as buyers expected further drops. Northern sellers cut Chinese SG-5 to 22,000 with few takers.
Chinese oil-based PVC to 24,000; Shintech SE950/SE1000 to 24,500, some accepting 24,200 - near Chinese levels, but trade was thin.
Veterans saw prices still falling this week, if by smaller steps.
